<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me identify all legacy patterns and modernize:

**Legacy patterns found:**

1. **Non-SDK-style project format** — Uses `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, explicit `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`
2. **Explicit `Compile` items** — SDK-style auto-globs `.cs` files
3. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style auto-generates assembly attributes from project properties
4. **Explicit framework references** (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) — Implicit in SDK-style


5. **Debug/Release configurations with redundant defaults** — SDK-style handles these automatically
6. **Unnecessary metadata** like `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, and `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` — All defaults in modern projects
7. **`TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2`** — Needs to become `TargetFramework net472`

I'm creating the modernized SDK-style project file...

Now I'm realizing that `OutputType` defaults to `Library` in SDK-style, so it can be removed. Same with `RootNamespace` and `AssemblyName` since they match the project filename. Let me strip those out and keep only the essential metadata properties.

data.content:
Here's a summary of every legacy pattern identified and what was removed/simplified:

| Legacy Pattern | Action |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, explicit `Import` of props/targets | → Replaced with `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `TargetFramework net472` |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="..."/>` items | → Removed; SDK-style auto-globs `*.cs` |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | → Deleted; metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`) |
| `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | → Removed (defaults in SDK-style) |
| `OutputType Library` | → Removed (default for SDK-style) |
| `RootNamespace` / `AssemblyName` matching project name | → Removed (inferred from filename) |
| Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | → Removed (all values were SDK defaults) |
| Explicit framework references (`System`, `System.Core`, etc.) | → Removed (implicit in SDK-style) |
| `Configuration` / `Platform` conditional properties | → Removed (SDK handles these) |

**Result:** 47 lines → 11 lines, with identical build semantics.
